title: Asuka and rape
wikitext:
[]: Asuka and rape             Quote Post by Coldwire » Wed Sep 12, 2012 4:06 pm Hi guys this is my first topic posted, I've been reading you guys for a long time and recently I decided to join the cult :). I can't help noticing that Asuka gets sort of sexually assaulted several times during the course of the series(not in a real way but on a symbolic way, well it was real on that ONE occasion) it seems to me like some sort of minor motive, do you think it has any significance? The scenes am referring to are: the mindrape, the hospital scene and the dismemberment by the MPE.

[]: Re: Asuka and rape             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Wed Sep 12, 2012 4:12 pm Coldwire wrote: Hi guys this is my first topic posted, I've been reading you guys for a long time and recently I decided to join the cult :). I can't help noticing that Asuka gets sort of sexually assaulted several times during the course of the series(not in a real way but on a symbolic way, well it was real on that ONE occasion) it seems to me like some sort of minor motive, do you think it has any significance? The scenes am referring to are: the mindrape, the hospital scene and the dismemberment by the MPE.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 12, 2012 4:23 pm Asuka gets mind-raped, then Rei gets tentacle raped, then Ritsuko gets vanilla.

[]:             Quote Post by Coldwire » Wed Sep 12, 2012 4:39 pm I know the mindrape wasn't actually sexual but I got the feeling it was intended to remind us of actual rape (I don't know, the way she asked it to stop... perhaps it was just me). Mr. Tines has a point though.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:12 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:12 pm Not to be rude, but i wouldn't call any of the mentioned situations (hospital scene, ep. 22 mental attack, and the MPE dismemberment) As sexual assault or even scenes with sexual undertones (except maybe ep 22) at all. Although that is probably just me. But yeah, like it was mentioned, sexuality is a big part of Asuka's character (similar to Misato i think) so maybe that played a role in it. And Ritsuko could have been raped by holograms. Somehow i imagine SEELE doing something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:15 pm Dream wrote: Not to be rude, but i wouldn't call any of the mentioned situations (hospital scene, ep. 22 mental attack, and the MPE dismemberment) As sexual assault or even scenes with sexual undertones (except maybe ep 22) at all. Although that is probably just me. But yeah, like it was mentioned, sexuality is a big part of Asuka's character (similar to Misato i think) so maybe that played a role in it. And Ritsuko could have been raped by holograms. Somehow i imagine SEELE doing something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:51 pm It's unclear anything was done to Ritsuko besides sexual humiliation in the form of having her appear in the nude with a not-so-subtle threat of further indignities. It's actually almost immaterial, because this wasn't the real torture - revealing that Gendo had betrayed her was. It's chilling, in a way, the levels of casual sadism SEELE are willing to descend to in pursuit of their goals. Dream : Shinji masturbating on Asuka is textbook sexual assault. As to the whole Asuka-as-a-fixation-of-sexual-abuse...eh, not sure. Yes, Shinji uses her in a casually sexual manner. I would also agree that the forced entry by Arael into Asuka's mind is designed to evoke rape (I suspect to give the viewer a context for how degrading and horrifying the experience is supposed to be). I wouldn't call the MPEs devouring Unit-02 sexual, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Rj123541 » Wed Sep 12, 2012 6:59 pm Der Kommissar wrote: Dream : Shinji masturbating on Asuka is textbook sexual assault.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Sep 12, 2012 7:00 pm I don't know if you've checked (highly recommended by the way) But, while i don't remember the specifics of the article too well, it had a strong case, with well-presented evidence for the rape allegations, including a special, layered phrasing in Kiel's original japanes dialogue that gets inevatibly lost in translation, at least from Japanese to English. But yeah the real torture was their casual comment on Gendo throwing her to the wolves, and SEELE are horrible indeed. Re: Shinji's hospital visit. I can understand why that is seen as textbook sexual assault, and on a logical point i think i can even agree. But, while it is undoubtebly wrong, i don't see it as "sexual assault" exactly. He isn't actively harming/punching/scratching her whatever, there isn't even any sort of physical contact in there. So i guess that's why it doesn't look like sexual assault to me. I can't say i agree on the idea of Arael's attack being inherently degrading or horrifying though.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 12, 2012 9:38 pm Dream wrote: Not to be rude, but i wouldn't call any of the mentioned situations (hospital scene, ep. 22 mental attack, and the MPE dismemberment) As sexual assault or even scenes with sexual undertones (except maybe ep 22) at all. Although that is probably just me.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Sep 13, 2012 3:56 am The hospital scene is almost certainly sexual assault by real-world legal definitions. He touched her without consent, took advantage of her incapacited state for his own sexual gratification, and more than likely did get some of his jizz on her.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Thu Sep 13, 2012 5:54 am Gendo considered it acceptable losses. That's why he sent her instead of Rei. The Evas were built. 01 had its needed S2 engine. 'The moor has done his duty. The moor can go.'

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Sep 13, 2012 6:25 am Dream wrote: Won't you happen to have a link to the EoE script by the way? I would like to see those sexual overtones notes you talk about.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Sep 13, 2012 6:37 am NemZ wrote: I think Reichu is the one who pointed that out to me. It's in the liner notes to the movie script, written in Japanese of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Tribblepoo » Thu Sep 13, 2012 7:18 am Dream wrote: On topic: It is an interesting premise, and i think there is something to it (NGE is a pretty subtle work, after all) But like i said, i don't exactly consider the mentioned scenes as a violation, at least not on a sexual way. It would be interesting actually to see if there are some subtleties or undertones that show how Asuka views sex and intimacy, aside from ep 22's violation.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Sep 13, 2012 7:21 am Dream wrote: Not to be rude, but i wouldn't call any of the mentioned situations (hospital scene, ep. 22 mental attack, and the MPE dismemberment) As sexual assault or even scenes with sexual undertones (except maybe ep 22) at all. Although that is probably just me.

[]: Rape inquiry. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Sep 13, 2012 7:50 am NemZ wrote: ...what? I'm not sure how that's even possible first of all,

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Sep 13, 2012 7:55 am I think it's pretty much been said that she just doesn't know what it means for her. Two things are fairly clear just from watching the series; Asuka has some pretty strict, "traditional" upbringing in regards to boys and girls and that she wants intimacy on a personal level, though not necessarily sexual. The issue is further complicated by the fact that she seems to be attracted to Shinji, but has an obvious distaste for being a mother (the reason is unclear, but it doubtlessly has to do with what her own mother did). There's no question that intimacy is closely tied to all the characters (fuck, the whole series is about some form of intimacy or lack of it) and that Asuka's aspect of such is sexual intimacy and the preconceptions and cultural notions that it carries. That includes rape, so far as it can be considered "sexual" in nature.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Sep 13, 2012 8:07 am Dream wrote: With "been said" you mean on the series? It has been some times since i last watched it so i don't remember too well. In any case it is indeed clear she has a rather old-fashioned idea of gender roles (at least when it comes to men) and that she yearns for a deep intimacy and support, probably even of an unconditional sort (She never had a mother or father after all) And while it often seems that she is attracted to Shinji, i always felt it kind of ambiguous, she might attracted to him or she might want to use him or who knows what. And i assume her distaste for being a mother is both because of her own mother, and that she simply doesn't want to be tied-up by another person.
